# Zed Wrapper Checklist: Zed Wrapper

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, completeness, and consistency of the Zed wrapper requirements before task generation
**Created**: 2026-07-03
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are Zed extension metadata requirements complete enough to identify the extension, its context server, and its local development purpose? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Contract `zed-extension.md`]
- [x] CHK002 Are preparation workflow requirements complete for both repository validation and local Zed development setup? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Plan §Technical Context]
- [x] CHK003 Are server artifact requirements defined for missing, stale, non-executable, and failed-on-start states? [Completeness, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §PreparedServerArtifact]
- [x] CHK004 Are launch profile requirements complete for command, arguments, working location, and environment allowlist? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004, Contract `launch-profile.md`]
- [x] CHK005 Are manual Zed smoke validation requirements documented separately from automated repository validation? [Completeness, Plan §Technical Context, Quickstart §Manual Zed Smoke Validation]
- [x] CHK006 Are publication, registry, provider, remote, and advanced UX exclusions documented consistently across spec, plan, and contracts? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, Plan §Summary, Research §Decision: Treat Zed MCP extension deprecation as a publication risk]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK007 Is "prepared server artifact" defined with clear identity, expected name, build mode, and usable states? [Clarity, Spec §Key Entities, Data Model §PreparedServerArtifact]
- [x] CHK008 Is "minimal allowlisted environment" defined with explicit allowed and rejected values rather than a general privacy statement? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010, Contract `launch-profile.md`]
- [x] CHK009 Is the meaning of "redacted diagnostic" specific enough to distinguish allowed status fields from forbidden sensitive content? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-009, Data Model §RedactedDiagnosticEvent]
- [x] CHK010 Is "local development extension" scoped clearly enough to avoid accidental marketplace or registry publication tasks? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Summary]
- [x] CHK011 Is the Zed host prerequisite caveat precise enough to guide tasks without authorizing global Rust or rustup installation? [Clarity, Plan §Constitution Check, Quickstart §Prerequisites]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK012 Do the spec and launch contract agree that the wrapper must not permit shell invocation, network retrieval, or HTTP/network transports? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, Contract `launch-profile.md`]
- [x] CHK013 Do the spec, plan, and Zed extension contract consistently preserve `translator-mcp` as the source of MCP behavior instead of duplicating translation logic? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, Plan §Structure Decision, Contract `zed-extension.md`]
- [x] CHK014 Do privacy requirements for logs, diagnostics, argv, and environment align with the constitution's provider boundary and log redaction rules? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009, Contract `launch-profile.md`, Constitution §Security And Privacy Requirements]
- [x] CHK015 Do no-mutation requirements cover buffers, source files, and selections consistently across user stories, functional requirements, and manual validation? [Consistency, Spec §US1, Spec §FR-007, Quickstart §No Mutation]
- [x] CHK016 Does the planned `zed-extension/` structure align with the prior repository-structure research and avoid conflict with Spec Kit directories? [Consistency, Plan §Project Structure, Research §Decision: Implement a local Zed MCP extension wrapper]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK017 Are startup failure criteria measurable enough to assess the 15 second visibility target without prescribing implementation mechanics? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004, Contract `launch-profile.md`]
- [x] CHK018 Are environment minimization outcomes objectively measurable from the requirements and contracts? [Measurability, Spec §SC-005, Quickstart §Environment Minimization]
- [x] CHK019 Are repeatability outcomes defined with observable absence of duplicate or conflicting generated state? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003, Contract `launch-profile.md`]
- [x] CHK020 Are diagnostic redaction outcomes objectively assessable across successful startup, failed startup, and failed translation? [Measurability, Spec §SC-006, Data Model §RedactedDiagnosticEvent]
- [x] CHK021 Are manual Zed smoke outcomes defined well enough to separate a valid product failure from a blocked host-prerequisite condition? [Acceptance Criteria, Quickstart §Manual Zed Smoke Validation]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK022 Are primary startup, preparation, and safe-diagnosis flows represented by independent user stories and matching contracts? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios, Contract `zed-extension.md`]
- [x] CHK023 Are exception requirements defined for unsupported context server ids and unsafe launch configuration, not only missing binary paths? [Coverage, Contract `launch-profile.md`]
- [x] CHK024 Are recovery or retry expectations documented for repeated registration, repeated startup, and restart after failed startup? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK025 Are host-prerequisite blocked states documented as requirements-quality inputs for planning and quickstart validation? [Coverage, Plan §Minimal host footprint, Quickstart §Prerequisites]
- [x] CHK026 Are remote/provider-denial scenarios represented at both extension configuration and MCP tool-call boundaries? [Coverage, Spec §FR-006, Quickstart §Remote Provider Denial]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK027 Are path-with-spaces and command-invocation edge cases addressed in requirements or explicitly deferred to task design? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK028 Are stale artifact and incompatible checkout cases defined with enough specificity to avoid ambiguous task decomposition? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §PreparedServerArtifact]
- [x] CHK029 Are unexpected inherited Zed, shell, token, and workspace variables covered as forbidden environment inputs? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Contract `launch-profile.md`]
- [x] CHK030 Are server warning, panic, provider error, and validation error diagnostics covered by the same redaction requirements? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-009]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK031 Are dependencies on completed MCP server contracts traceable from this feature's requirements to `specs/002-mcp-server/` artifacts? [Dependency, Spec §FR-005, Contract `launch-profile.md`]
- [x] CHK032 Are assumptions about Zed installation and local dev-extension prerequisites separated from project-managed dependencies? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Quickstart §Prerequisites]
- [x] CHK033 Are project Make targets named in quickstart requirements without implying they already exist before task generation? [Clarity, Quickstart §Planned Repository Validation Commands]
- [x] CHK034 Are supply-chain and lockfile expectations for adding `zed_extension_api` covered by plan-level dependency requirements? [Dependency, Plan §Technical Context, Constitution §Minimal Host Footprint]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK035 Is there any unresolved conflict between Zed's Rust/rustup dev-extension prerequisite and the project's no-global-runtime host policy? [Conflict, Plan §Constitution Check, Quickstart §Prerequisites]
- [x] CHK036 Is the allowed `binary_path` setting clearly distinguished from forbidden arbitrary paths, provider config, and environment overrides? [Ambiguity, Contract `zed-extension.md`, Contract `launch-profile.md`]
- [x] CHK037 Are failure categories stable and sufficiently mapped to user-actionable messages without exposing implementation details? [Ambiguity, Contract `launch-profile.md`]
- [x] CHK038 Are requirements clear about what remains deferred to F007 and F009 so tasks do not pull in UX or publication work? [Boundary, Spec §FR-006, Research §Decision: Treat Zed MCP extension deprecation as a publication risk]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
- Reviewed on 2026-07-03: all 38 requirement-quality checks are satisfied after tightening stale/incompatible artifact, repeated-startup, path-with-spaces, and lockfile/dependency wording in the feature artifacts.
- Amended 2026-07-04: CHK017's original wording ("the 15 second visibility target") predates the SC-004 amendment. `spec.md` SC-004 and `contracts/launch-profile.md` now target visibility within the Zed context-server initialization window (~60s observed) instead of 15 seconds, after confirming no faster WASM-sandbox-compatible primitive exists in `zed_extension_api` 0.7.0. CHK017 remains satisfied under the amended criterion: the target is still measurable and does not prescribe implementation mechanics.
